description: Large fragment of a late legal document. In Hebrew. The paleography supports a dating estimate of late 18th- or early 19th-century, possibly the scribal hand of Meʾīr Ben-Naʿīm or one of his contemporaries in Cairo. Seʿadya Amariyo known as Simana/Semana acknowledges that he owes 18 reales, each equivalent to 90 silver muayyadis (of acct.), to his sister Teḥila bt. Raḥamim Amariyo. ASE/MCD
